key_insight: An ML-enhanced multi-minima iterative genetic algorithm can discover both ground-state and metastable crystal structures without relying on existing structural databases.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable inverse design of complex materials, especially when the target phase sits in a rugged energy landscape with strong chemical incompatibilities.
method: Combine an ANN-based interatomic potential with a genetic algorithm search.
Use an iterative penalty scheme inspired by metadynamics to avoid revisiting previously found minima.
Map both global minima and competing metastable phases for a given composition.
evidence_signal: On the La-Co-Pb system, the method predicted the Pbam ground state of La4Co4Pb and exactly matched the independently XRD-determined orthorhombic structure of La5CoPb2 using only composition information.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and transferability of the ANN potential, so difficult chemistries or sparse training data could still limit reliability.
paper_title: Complex crystal structure prediction using ML-enhanced multi-minima iterative genetic algorithm
